fixtures.name: paradigm_break_xhr
fixtures.rationale: ky is entirely fetch-based; XMLHttpRequest is never used in the source

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_callback
fixtures.rationale: ky always returns a ResponsePromise; Node.js-style error-first callbacks are never used

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_class_client
fixtures.rationale: ky exposes a factory function (ky.create/ky.extend), never a class that callers instantiate with new

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_explicit_promise
fixtures.rationale: ky never wraps fetch in new Promise(); it chains directly on the fetch Promise or uses async/await

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_interceptors
fixtures.rationale: ky uses plain hooks arrays on a hooks object; axios-style client.interceptors.request.use() is never present

fixtures.name: control_options_normalization
fixtures.rationale: Idiomatic ky options normalization: plain object spread, new Headers(), hooks array merging, retry/timeout defaults

fixtures.name: control_response_handling
fixtures.rationale: Idiomatic ky ResponsePromise pattern: response.clone(), status check throwing HTTPError, typed .json<T>() / .text() shortcuts
